title: Static subpages under site/ must use ABSOLUTE paths — cleanUrls+trailingSlash:false breaks relative refs
context: The Hunch blog (site/blog/) shipped with relative refs (<script src=posts.js>, href=post.html?slug=…) and the posts didn't render on the deployed site.
decision: Root cause: vercel.json sets cleanUrls:true + trailingSlash:false, so site/blog/index.html is served at the URL /blog (no trailing slash). A browser resolves a relative URL against /blog's parent directory (/), so <script src="posts.js"> requested /posts.js → 404 → window.POSTS undefined → zero posts rendered. (It worked on the post page, served at /blog/post, whose base dir IS /blog/.) Fix: every asset and inter-page link on a static subpage must be ROOT-ABSOLUTE — /blog/posts.js, /blog/post?slug=…, /, /docs, /blog — not relative and not ../.
consequences: Any NEW static page added under site/ in a subdirectory must use absolute paths for scripts/links, or it will silently 404 its assets on the deployed site (while working locally via file://, which masks the bug).
Clean URLs in links: use /blog/post?slug=x (the clean form) not /blog/post.html?slug=x to avoid a redirect hop; cleanUrls maps /blog/post → /blog/post.html and preserves the query string.
Validate a static page's render by executing its inline script in node with window/document shims (catches runtime errors that a parse-only --check misses).
